sentence_reviews.text: Reward-model-based RLHF fits a preference predictor and optimizes a policy against it, usually with a divergence penalty limiting drift from a reference model.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang arXiv abstract lines 38-40 describes collecting output rankings and further fine-tuning a supervised model using RLHF; DPO arXiv abstract lines 42-44 describes RLHF first fitting a reward model reflecting human preferences and then using RL while avoiding drift from the original model.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is directly supported by the RLHF and DPO abstracts.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DPO arXiv abstract lines 42-44 describes RLHF first fitting a reward model reflecting human preferences; Ouyang lines 38-40 describes ranking outputs and RLHF.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Reward modeling, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.
